Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 146. City Rulers Petition the King (ABL 0136)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL EN-ia

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Šamaš-upahhir. Good health to the king, my lord!

o 22

ARAD-ka mdUTU—ú-pa-ḫír

o 33

lu-u DI-mu a-na LUGAL EN-ia

o 44

ARAD-MEŠ-ni ša LUGAL EN-ia

o 55

LÚv.EN—URU*-MEŠ-ni ša ŠU.2-ia

(5) The king my lord's servants, the city-rulers under my authority whom the king [my lord] o[rdered] to work in Milqia [have co]me [to my presence saying: "......

o 66

ša LUGAL [be-lí ina] ⸢ŠÀ⸣ URU.mil-qi-a

o 77

⸢dul-lu*⸣ [a-na e-pa-a]-še

o 88

⸢iq⸣-[bu-u]-⸢ni⸣

o 99

[ina pa-ni-ia it-tal-ku]-⸢ni⸣

at least 10 lines broken away

(Break)

Reverse
rr beginning broken away

r 1'1'

[ma-a ina É].GAL* šu*-⸢pur*⸣ [x x]

(r 1) "Send [..... to the pa]lace!"

r 2'2'

[x ša] ⸢LUGAL⸣ EN-ia

(r 2) They have made [me] sack [the ... of the ki]ng, my lord; what does the king my lord say?

r 3'3'

ú-sa-ap-ti-[u-ni]

r 4'4'

mi-i-nu ša LUGAL be-lí

r 5'5'

i-qa-ab-bu-u-ni

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334083/.
